evidence: All-URLs content script page/video metadata flows through type-only background validation to desktop URL resolution and yt-dlp process spawn without a destination allowlist/private-network filter.

method: Full-file review plus focused vitest evidence
evidence: Focused command passed. subtitleService.test.ts:104 demonstrates getSubtitles accepts http://video.local/watch. connectionManager.test.ts:195-210 demonstrates unknown-site videoSrc fallback is a supported flow. Code review confirms resolveVideoUrl allows any http(s) pageUrl/videoSrc and subtitleService spawns yt-dlp with that URL.
proof_gap: No network harness was run to demonstrate a blind SSRF against a live private endpoint; validation should calibrate exploitability and data-return limits.

source: Malicious page or frame with a qualifying video observed by the all-URLs content script
broken_control: No destination allowlist, private/LAN/loopback block, redirect policy, or explicit user intent check before desktop fetch
sink: desktop subtitleService builds yt-dlp args with the resolved URL and spawns yt-dlp
impact: Browser page can cause the local desktop app to issue HTTP(S) requests and run yt-dlp against attacker-selected media URLs; default args include browser-cookie extraction, increasing privacy sensitivity
preconditions: Desktop app is connected to the extension
The page or frame can produce a media snapshot passing readyState and minimum-duration filtering
severity_facts: Crosses untrusted page to desktop process/network boundary
Command injection is not the claim because spawn uses argv, not a shell
Response exfiltration was not proven in this subtask
